nativeRange.habitat: Marshes, swamps, ditches, and pond margins of subtropical to tropical lowland wetlands; ranges from the southeastern United States (Texas, Florida, north to Iowa) through Mexico, the West Indies, and Central America south to Paraguay

summary: A large background sword native from the southern U.S. and Mexico south to Paraguay; the 'Marble Queen' cultivar carries striking cream-and-green marbled, heart-based leaves that make it a centerpiece for medium-to-large planted tanks.
careNotes: Echinodorus cordifolius is a robust sword plant from subtropical and tropical American wetlands, distinguished in cultivation by the 'Marble Queen' (also marketed as 'Radican Marble Queen') cultivar with cream-and-green marbled leaves. Submerged adult plants in the hobby typically reach 35–55 cm (Aqua-Fish.net), with Modern Aquarium describing large robust rosettes and Wikipedia noting wild emersed specimens up to 100 cm — the 35–60 cm range used here brackets typical submerged aquarium growth. Temperature 22–28 °C (71–82 °F) is consistent between Modern Aquarium and Aqua-Fish.net. pH 6.2–7.5 and gH 4–12 are from Aqua-Fish.net, slightly broadened on the high pH end to reflect the plant's known tolerance of harder water. Lighting demand is medium-to-high: Modern Aquarium recommends moderate–strong light, Wikipedia notes strong lighting for best growth, and Aqua-Fish.net specifies bright conditions; under low light the cultivar's marbling fades. CO2 is not strictly required (Modern Aquarium) but is beneficial in brighter setups and supports the larger leaf form (Wikipedia). This is a HEAVY ROOT FEEDER — Modern Aquarium emphasises root tabs and iron-rich fertilization, and a deep nutrient-rich substrate is needed for the mature root mass. Place as a midground specimen in large tanks, or as a single background centerpiece in medium-sized tanks (60–75 cm). Like other Echinodorus species the plant grows readily emersed (its natural form, with broad cordate leaves above water) and submerged (longer, more lance-like leaves); plants arriving in their emersed form often shed initial leaves before adapting to submerged growth, which is normal and not a sign of poor health.
breedingNotes: Propagates through crown division and through adventitious plantlets ('daughter plants') that form around the base of the mother plant and along long flower/runner stalks; once each plantlet has a small rosette and visible roots it can be cut free and planted in the substrate. In wild and emersed setups the species flowers freely from spring through fall and can set seed, but flowering and seed production are uncommon in fully submerged aquarium conditions.

primary.name: Modern Aquarium
primary.notes: Care page for Radican Marble Queen (Echinodorus cordifolius). Provided North American / Mexican native origin, temperature 71–82 °F (~22–28 °C), moderate–strong lighting, CO2 optional, moderate-to-fast growth under good conditions, midground/background placement, heavy root feeder needing iron-rich fertilization and nutrient-dense substrate, easy care level.

additional.name: Wikipedia — Echinodorus cordifolius
additional.notes: Used for taxonomy (family Alismataceae, order Alismatales), full native range (Mexico, West Indies, Central America, South America to Paraguay, southeastern US north to Iowa), habitat (marshes, swamps, ditches, ponds), wild size to 100 cm, emersed-dominant growth form, and confirmation of 'Marble Queen' marbled-leaf cultivar that fades under low light. Notes that strong lighting and CO2 support best growth.

additional.name: Aqua-Fish.net — Echinodorus cordifolius
additional.notes: Filled water-parameter gaps left by Modern Aquarium: pH 6.2–7.1, hardness 4–12 dGH, temperature 22–28 °C. Submerged size 35–55 cm, steady growth, midground placement, easy care level, propagation via crown division and adventitious plantlets around the rosette base. Notes initial leaf-shedding on introduction is normal.
